title: allium moly subsp. massaessylum
intro: Allium moly subsp. massaessylum, also known as the golden garlic, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its sulfur compounds and other bioactive molecules. The golden garlic has been used to treat various health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive issues, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Allium moly subsp. massaessylum include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
